Niche.title: Niche
Niche.description: Represents a specific niche offered by the company (e.g., Data Entry, Social Media Manager).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Niche entity.

name.description: Name of the niche (e.g., Data Entry).

imageUri.description: URI for the image representing the niche.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Represents a project example within a specific niche.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Project entity.

imageUris.description: URIs for the images associated with the project.

liveUrl.description: URL to the live version of the project, if applicable.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Testimonial entity.

name.description: Name of the client providing the testimonial.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the testimonial was created.

ContactFormSubmission.title: ContactFormSubmission
ContactFormSubmission.description: Represents a submission from the contact form.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactFormSubmission entity.

name.description: Name of the person submitting the form.

email.description: Email address of the person submitting the form.

message.description: The message content from the contact form.

submissionDate.description: Date when the form was submitted.

AboutSection.title: AboutSection
AboutSection.description: Represents the content for the about section.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AboutSection entity.

content.description: Detailed content of the about section.

imageUri.description: URI for the image associated with the about section.

definition.description: Stores all Niche entities. Public read access.
Stores all Project entities related to a specific niche. Public read access.

params.name: nicheId
params.description: The ID of the niche the project belongs to.

definition.description: Stores all Testimonial entities. Public read and create access.
Stores all ContactFormSubmission entities. Public write access, restricted read access.
Stores AboutSection entities. Public read access.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to optimize for security, scalability, and ease of querying, following the principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling. Each entity is stored in its own top-level collection or within a user-scoped collection (if applicable), ensuring clear separation of concerns and simplified security rules.

*   **niches:** Stores Niche entities.  Since niches are global data, it resides in the top-level collection `/niches`. Read access is public.
*   **niches/{nicheId}/projects:** Stores Project entities. A subcollection under each Niche, allowing easy access to projects related to a specific niche.  Read access is public.
*   **testimonials:** Stores Testimonial entities. Public read and create access to allow users to submit and view testimonials.
*   **contact_form_submissions:** Stores ContactFormSubmission entities.  Write access is public (no auth). Read access should be restricted.
*   **about_sections:** Stores AboutSection entities. Since there will be only one about section it is preferable to use a document with a known ID. However, using a collection allows more flexibility in the future. Read access is public.

This structure facilitates straightforward security rules. For instance, listing testimonials for a specific niche can be done securely without complex `get()` calls due to the denormalized `nicheId` within each testimonial document.  The segregation of data types into separate collections simplifies querying and indexing.
